steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure of the modifier
steps.content:
The blank requires a word followed by a modifier that describes the fertilizers.

The key question is whether the modifier specifies which fertilizers (restrictive) or adds extra information about already-identified fertilizers (nonrestrictive).

The structure is: “agricultural [noun] [modifier] macronutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium.” A restrictive modifier (no comma) specifies which fertilizers; a nonrestrictive modifier (comma) adds extra info.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive vs. nonrestrictive rule to the choices
steps.content:
C is correct. The study investigates the relationship with fertilizers that specifically contain these macronutrients—not fertilizers in general.

Choice C uses “fertilizers containing” (no comma), a restrictive participial phrase that specifies which type of fertilizers.

This is the most accurate representation of the study's focus.

Choices B and D add commas, making the modifier nonrestrictive and suggesting the fertilizers are already identified, which softens the specificity.

Choice A creates a sentence fragment (“Containing macronutrients...”) after the period.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
